similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths in al-Kafi or other sources where the Imam declares that scholars who transmit his teachings have been granted authority to issue legal rulings on his behalf, expanding the concept of delegated authority to jurists.
Quranic verses and hadiths about the Taghut — unjust ruling powers that believers are commanded to reject and not submit to, including reference to Quran 4:60 which is explicitly invoked here.

Hadiths establishing that the Imams' companions who relay their teachings occupy a special position of religious authority, with the rejection of their valid rulings being treated as a rejection of the Imams themselves and ultimately of God.

summaries.bn: এটি উমার ইবনে হানযালার বিখ্যাত 'মাকবুলা' হাদিস, যা শিয়া ফিকহে ওলায়াতে ফকিহ মতবাদের অন্যতম মূল ভিত্তি। ইমাম সাদিক (আ.) অবৈধ কর্তৃপক্ষ (তাগুত) থেকে বিচার নিতে নিষেধ করেছেন এবং যোগ্য ফকিহকে তাঁর প্রতিনিধি হিসেবে নিয়োগ করেছেন।
summaries.de: Dies ist der berühmte 'Maqbula'-Hadith von Umar ibn Hanzala, einer der grundlegenden Texte der schiitischen Jurisprudenz für die Doktrin der Wilayat al-Faqih. Imam Sadiq (F) verbietet schiitischen Muslimen, Urteile von illegitimen Herrschaftsbehörden (dem 'Taghut') einzuholen, und delegiert richterliche Autorität an qualifizierte Juristen als seine Vertreter.
summaries.en: This is the famous 'maqbula' hadith of Umar ibn Hanzala, one of the foundational texts for the doctrine of wilayat al-faqih (guardianship of the jurist) in Shia jurisprudence. Imam Sadiq (AS) forbids Shia Muslims from seeking judgment from unjust ruling authorities (the 'Taghut'), even when their rights are legally valid, because accepting such a judgment through illegitimate channels nullifies its moral basis. In the second part, the Imam provides an alternative: seek a qualified jurist who has studied the Imams' teachings, and he carries delegated judicial authority — rejecting his rulings based on Imamic law is tantamount to rejecting God Himself.
summaries.es: Este es el famoso hadiz 'maqbula' de Umar ibn Hanzala, uno de los textos fundamentales de la jurisprudencia chií para la doctrina de la wilayat al-faqih. El Imam Sadiq (P) prohíbe a los chiíes buscar juicio de autoridades ilegítimas (el 'Taghut') y delega autoridad judicial a los juristas cualificados como sus representantes.
summaries.fa: این حدیث مشهور 'مقبوله عمر بن حنظله' یکی از مهم‌ترین مستندات فقهی برای نظریه ولایت فقیه در فقه شیعه است. امام صادق علیه السلام مراجعه به قضات و حکام غیر مشروع (طاغوت) را ممنوع دانسته و فقهای واجد شرایط را به عنوان نمایندگان خود در امر قضاوت معرفی فرموده‌اند.
summaries.fr: Il s'agit du célèbre hadith 'maqbula' d'Umar ibn Hanzala, l'un des textes fondateurs de la jurisprudence chiite pour la doctrine de la wilayat al-faqih. L'imam Sadiq (P) interdit aux chiites de solliciter le jugement des autorités illégitimes (le 'Taghut') et délègue l'autorité judiciaire aux juristes qualifiés en tant que ses représentants.
summaries.id: Ini adalah hadits 'maqbula' Umar bin Hanzala yang terkenal, salah satu teks paling penting dalam fiqih Syiah untuk doktrin wilayat al-faqih. Imam Shadiq (a.s.) melarang kaum Syiah mencari keputusan dari penguasa yang tidak sah dan mengangkat ulama yang memenuhi syarat sebagai wakilnya dalam urusan peradilan.
summaries.ru: Это знаменитый хадис 'Мактула' Умара ибн Ханзалы — один из основополагающих текстов в шиитской юриспруденции для доктрины вилаят аль-факих. Имам Садик (мир ему) запрещает шиитам обращаться за судебным решением к незаконным властям (Тагуту) и делегирует судебные полномочия квалифицированным юристам как своим представителям.
summaries.tr: Bu, Ömer b. Hanzala'nın meşhur 'makbule' hadisidir. Şia fıkhında velayeti faqih doktrininin temel dayanaklarından olan bu hadiste İmam Sadık (a.s.) Müslümanları tağuta (gayri meşru otorite) başvurmaktan men etmiş ve nitelikli âlimleri kendi yargı temsilcileri olarak tayin etmiştir.
summaries.ur: یہ عمر بن حنظلہ کی مشہور 'مقبولہ' حدیث ہے جو شیعہ فقہ میں ولایتِ فقیہ کی بنیادی دلیل ہے۔ امام صادق علیہ السلام نے ناجائز حکومتوں (طاغوت) سے فیصلہ لینے سے منع فرمایا اور اہل علم فقیہ کو اپنا نمائندہ مقرر فرمایا۔
summaries.zh: 这是欧麦尔·本·罕扎拉著名的'马克布拉'哈迪斯，是什叶派法学中法学家监护权（威拉亚特·法基赫）学说的基础文献之一。伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）禁止什叶派穆斯林向非法权威（塔古特）寻求判决，并将司法权力委托给合格的法学家作为其代表。

translation.es: y acudieron ambos a un juicio
translation.fr: ils ont tous deux demandé un jugement
translation.ur: پس انہوں نے فیصلے کے لیے رجوع کیا
فیصلے کے لیے رجوع کرتا ہے

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/275)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا يَكُونُ بَيْنَهُمَا مُنَازَعَةٌ فِي دَيْنٍ أَوْ مِيرَاثٍ فَتَحَاكَمَا إِلَى السُّلْطَانِ أَوْ إِلَى الْقُضَاةِ أَ يَحِلُّ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ مَنْ تَحَاكَمَ إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ فَحَكَمَ لَهُ فَإِنَّمَا يَأْخُذُ سُحْتاً وَ إِنْ كَانَ حَقُّهُ ثَابِتاً لِأَنَّهُ أَخَذَ بِحُكْمِ الطَّاغُوتِ وَ قَدْ أَمَرَ اللَّهُ أَنْ يُكْفَرَ بِهِ

قُلْتُ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعَانِ قَالَ انْظُرُوا إِلَى مَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ قَدْ رَوَى حَدِيثَنَا وَ نَظَرَ فِي حَلَالِنَا وَ حَرَامِنَا وَ عَرَفَ أَحْكَامَنَا فَارْضَوْا بِهِ حَكَماً فَإِنِّي قَدْ جَعَلْتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ حَاكِماً فَإِذَا حَكَمَ بِحُكْمِنَا فَلَمْ يَقْبَلْهُ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّمَا بِحُكْمِ اللَّهِ قَدِ اسْتَخَفَّ وَ عَلَيْنَا رَدَّ وَ الرَّادُّ عَلَيْنَا الرَّادُّ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى حَدِّ الشِّرْكِ بِاللَّهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Safwan, from Dawood Bin Al Husayn, from Umar Bin Hanzala who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about two men from our companions who happened to have a dispute between them regarding a debt, or inheritance. So they went to the Sultan (ruling authority) for judgement, or to the judge. Is that permissible?’ So heasws said: ‘The one who goes for a judgement to the tyrant, so he judges for him, so he has rather taken ill-gotten gains, even if it was his right, proved, because he has taken it by a judgement of the tyrant. And Allahazwj has Commanded that [4:60] though they were Commanded to deny him’.

I said, ‘How should they both deal with it?’ Heasws said: ‘They should look around to the one who was among them who has reported ourasws Hadeeth, and has looked into ourasws Permissibles, and ourasws Prohibition, and recognises ourasws judgements. So they should present to him for the judgement, for Iasws have made him to be a judge over you. So when he judges by ourasws judgements, and it is not accepted from him, so rather it is the Judgement of Allahazwj which has been taken lightly, and against usasws is the rejection, and the rejection against usasws is the rejection against Allahazwj, and he (the rejecter) would be upon a legal penalty (Hadd) of the association with Allahazwj’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Safwan from Dawud ibn al- Haseen from ‘Umar ibn Hanzalah who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about two of our people who have a dispute over a certain issue of religion or inheritance, and they go for judgment before the Sultan or a judge; if it is lawful. He (the Imam) said, ‘Whoever seeks judgment before al-Taghut (transgressors) who judges for him, whatever he then receives by such judgment is al-suht (sinful earning) even if it is his established right; he has taken it through the judgment of al-Taghut (transgressors) when Allah has commanded to reject al-Taghut.’ I then asked, ’What then should they do?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘You must find one among you who narrates our Hadith and has given thought about our lawful and unlawful matters and has learned our rules; then you must accept him as the judge among you; I have made him the judge and ruler over you. When he issues judgments according to our rules and it is not accepted from him, then the rule and laws of Allah are taken lightly and our rules are rejected. Those who reject us, they have rejected Allah and that is like taking things as partners of Allah.’”
